codeReview.summary: Fail: the feature lands a transcript-backed text surface rather than integrating an authoritative live SSH/PTY terminal surface, so it does not satisfy the contract for real remote IO, special-key behavior, or representative full-screen terminal programs.

issues.description: The session UI rebuilds terminal state from `sessionState.transcript` via `terminalStateHolder.applyTranscript(...)` instead of binding the terminal surface to live SSH/PTY state. That means the new surface is still a transcript reconstruction layer, so copy/paste and special-key actions are not proven against authoritative remote terminal state and the feature does not meet the required end-to-end terminal behavior.

The chosen terminal rendering approach was supposed to wrap a dedicated terminal view/emulator, but this implementation renders a `LazyColumn` of plain text lines. Combined with the minimal escape-sequence handling in `TerminalBuffer`, this is not a real terminal surface and will not support representative full-screen terminal programs or broader ANSI/TUI behavior required by the feature contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `ssh-terminal-worker` skill does not currently tell workers how to handle a terminal feature that cannot yet be completed end-to-end without violating the architecture. In this run the worker knowingly landed a transcript-backed intermediate slice even though the skill requires real interactive terminal checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `2026-04-11T06-17-52-513Z__terminal-core-rendering-and-input__ff6ece82-3462-43b3-87e2-a671ec94255f.json` reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false`, says live SSH terminal checks were skipped because the surface was not driven by real SSH IO, and suggests clarifying how to report partial architectural progress.

summary: Reviewed commit 558ef02 and the worker transcript skeleton. The feature fails review because it delivers a transcript-backed Compose text surface instead of the required live SSH/PTY-backed terminal integration, and it reimplements terminal rendering as plain Compose text rather than the documented dedicated terminal-emulator boundary.
